service.description: Azure Data Factory appears across 31 normalized findings in 3 checklist families. 3 lower-confidence families broaden design coverage.
service.whatThisMeans: This service is covered mainly by preview or mixed-confidence guidance. Use it for specialist review with explicit validation.

quality.label: Preview · Medium confidence
quality.summary: Use with architectural judgment. Preview content is valuable, but it should not be treated as equivalent to mature review baselines.

quality.label: Preview · Medium confidence
quality.summary: Use with architectural judgment. Preview content is valuable, but it should not be treated as equivalent to mature review baselines.

quality.label: Preview · Medium confidence
quality.summary: Use with architectural judgment. Preview content is valuable, but it should not be treated as equivalent to mature review baselines.

items.text: Use zone redundant pipelines in regions that support Availability Zones
Restrict use of local users whereever necessary
items.description: Restrict the use of local authentication methods for data plane access. Instead, use Microsoft Entra ID as the default authentication method to control your data plane access.

items.text: Separate and limit highly privileged/administrative users and enable MFA and conditional policies
items.description: If not required for routine administrative operations, disable or restrict any local admin accounts for only emergency use.

items.text: Store passwords, secrets in Azure Key Vault
Backup your code with DevOps
Plan for Disaster recovery using Active/Active or Active/Passive Configuration
Reference Databricks HA/DR playbook
Leverage FTA Resiliency Playbook for Azure Data Factory
Make sure you replicate or duplicate your network in the sister region. You have to make a copy of your Vnet in another region
Make sure you replicate the Self-Hosted Integration Runtime VMs in another region
Use DevOps to Backup the ARM templates with Github/Azure DevOps integration
Configure managed private endpoints to connect to resources using managed azure IR
items.description: Managed private endpoints are private endpoints created in the Data Factory managed virtual network that establishes a private link to Azure resources. Data Factory manages these private endpoints on your behalf.

items.text: Configure Private Links to connect to sources in customer Vnet and data factory
items.description: By using Azure Private Link, you can connect to various platform as a service (PaaS) deployments in Azure via a private endpoint. A private endpoint is a private IP address within a specific virtual network and subnet

items.text: Data Encryption at rest by Microsoft managed keys
Data Encryption in transit by BYOK (Customer managed keys)
items.description: When you specify a customer-managed key, Data Factory uses�both�the factory system key and the CMK to encrypt customer data. Missing either would result in Deny of Access to data and factory.

items.text: Data Encryption in transit by Microsoft managed keys
Deploy SHIR VMs in your vnet if you are working with sensitive data that shouldn�t leave your corporate network
Disable access over public internet and configure either firewall rules or trusted services rules
Encrypt credentials for on-premises using SHIR data stores in Azure Data Factory
items.description: You can encrypt and store credentials for any of your on-premises data stores (linked services with sensitive information) on a machine with self-hosted integration runtime.

items.text: Use Azure Key Vault secrets in pipeline activities
items.description: You can store credentials or secret values in an Azure Key Vault and use them during pipeline execution to pass to your activities.

items.text: Use Azure Key Vault secrets in pipeline activities
items.description: You can store credentials or secret values in an Azure Key Vault and use them during pipeline execution to pass to your activities.

items.text: Use managed identity to authenticate to the services
items.description: Managed identities eliminate the need to manage credentials. Managed identities provide an identity for the service instance when connecting to resources that support Microsoft Entra authentication.

items.text: Use managed vnet IR to restrict the access over public internet for Azure Integration Runtime
items.description: When you create an Azure integration runtime within a Data Factory managed virtual network, the integration runtime is provisioned with the managed virtual network. It uses private endpoints to securely connect to supported data stores.

items.text: Backup your data to Azure Storage RA-GRS
items.description: Download the blob using the secondary endpoint in RAGRS storage account

items.text: Backup your data with deep and shallow clones
Backup your workspace configuration including ARM templates and secret scopes
Plan Disaster Recovery strategy in Databricks using the Hive External Metastore
Share metaData across different Databricks workspaces using Hive external metastore
Use Databricks Migration tools
items.description: Migration package to log all Databricks resources for backup and/or migrating to another Databricks workspace

items.text: If using Keyvault integration, use SLA of Keyvault to understand your availablity
items.description: If your ADF Pipelines use Key Vault you don't have to do anything to replicate Key Vault. Key Vault is a managed service and Microsoft takes care of it for you

items.text: Use Databricks Sync
